title: Computer-implemented method for creating a 3d-printing file of at least one dental aligner for dental self-treatment of a customer based on at least one digital image of the customer's teeth, and dental aligner, in particular 3d-printed
abstract: The present invention relates to a computer-implemented method for creating a 3D-printing file of at least one dental aligner for dental self-treatment of a customer based on at least one digital image of the customer's teeth, and to a data processing system for carrying out the steps of the method. In order to improve the efficiency and precision of the production of customer-adapted dental aligners with a minimum of resources and interaction of dentists with customers and collaboration with dental laboratories, the present invention provides a computer-implemented method for creating at least one 3D-printing file of at least one dental aligner for dental self-treatment of a customer based on at least one digital image of the customer's teeth, wherein the method includes the following steps: Step A: Verifying whether or not the at least one digital image satisfies preset requirements on image eligibility and/or customer eligibility. Step B: If the verification in Step A is positive, creating the at least one 3D-printing file for 3D- printing the at least one dental aligner based on the at least one digital image. A data processing system for carrying out the steps of the method, a dental aligner, in particular 3D-printed, and a communication system comprising said dental aligner are also disclosed.
claims:
Claims 1. Computer-implemented method for creating at least one 3D-printing file of at least one dental aligner for dental self-treatment of a customer based on at least one digital image of the customerâs teeth, wherein the method includes the following steps: a. Step A (100): Verifying whether or not the at least one digital image satisfies preset requirements on customer eligibility, wherein Step A includes: i. Sub-Step A5-1 (151): Setting a parameter requirement for at least one cus tomer eligibility parameter representative of an eligible customer for dental self-treatment with the at least one dental aligner, said customer eligibility parameter requirement being preferably derived from a catalogue of images of eligible customers. ii. Sub-Step A5-2 (152): Parameterizing the at least one digital image so as to determine at least one parameter value from the at least one digital image for enabling comparison with the parameter requirement set in Sub-Step A5-1 (151), preferably using object recognition technology. iii. Sub-Step A5-3 (153): Verifying whether or the at least one parameter value determined in Sub-Step A5-2 (152) complies with the parameter require ment set in Sub-Step A5-1 (151). b. Step B (200): If the verification in Step A is positive, creating the at least one 3D- printing file for 3D-printing the at least one dental aligner based on the at least one digital image. 2. Computer-implemented method according to claim 1 , characterized in that Step A com prises: a. Sub-Step A4 (140): Verifying whether or not the at least one digital image satisfies preset image eligibility requirements on image quality and/or image content, includ ing the following Sub-Steps: i. Sub-Step A4-1 (141): Setting a parameter requirement for at least one im age eligibility parameter representative of eligible image quality and for at 

 least one image eligibility parameter representative of eligible image con tent, said image eligibility parameter requirement being preferably derived from a catalogue of eligible images. ii. Sub-Step A4-2 (142): Parameterizing the at least one digital image so as to determine at least one parameter value from the at least one digital image for enabling comparison with the parameter requirement set in Sub-Step A4-1 (141). iii. Sub-Step A4-3 (143): Verifying whether or not the at least one parameter value determined in Sub-Step A4-2 (142) complies with the parameter re quirement set in Sub-Step A4-1 (141). iv. Sub-Step A4-5 (145): If the verification in Step A4-3 is positive, proceeding with the method, preferably with Sub-Step A5 (150). 3. Computer-implemented method according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that Step A further comprises: a. Sub-Step A6 (160): Simulating the effects of the dental self-treatment of the cus tomer based on the at least one digital image of the customerâs teeth, including the following Sub-Steps: i. Sub-Step A6-1 (161): Computing a target position and/or orientation of at least two teeth or of all teeth of the upper jaws and/or lower jaws of the customer representing a target state upon completion of dental self-treat ment with the at least one aligner (1), preferably based on the at least one digital image. ii. Sub-Step A6-2 (162): Computing at least one intermediate position and/or orientation of teeth of the customer representing an intermediate state dur ing dental treatment with the at least one aligner based on the at least one digital image. iii. Sub-Step A6-3 (163): Generating at least one simulated view of the teeth of the customer during or after completion of the dental self-treatment based on the computation in Sub-Step A6-1 and Sub-Step A6-2 on the basis of 

 the at least on digital image by modifying the at least on digital image based on the computation in Sub-Step A6-1 and/or Sub-Step A6-2. iv. Sub-Step A6-5 (165): Developing a treatment plan based on the computa tion in Sub-Step A6-1 and/or Sub-Step A6-2. 4. Computer-implemented method according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that Step A further comprises: a. Sub-Step A7 (170): Assessing a progress of the dental self-treatment based on at least one in-treatment or post-treatment digital image of the customerâs teeth made during or after the dental self-treatment, including the following Sub-Steps: i. Sub-Step A7-2 (172): Executing Sub-Step A4 (140) on the at least one in treatment or post-treatment digital image of the customerâs teeth. ii. Sub-Step A7-3 (173): Setting a parameter requirement for at least one treat ment progress parameter representative of an acceptable treatment pro gress, said treatment progress parameter requirement being derived from a catalogue of images related to customers with successful treatments. iii. Sub-Step A7-4 (174): Parameterizing the at least one in-treatment or post treatment digital image so as to determine at least one treatment progress parameter value from the at least one in-treatment or post-treatment digital image, and, if applicable, the at least one pre-treatment digital image of the customerâs teeth made before the dental self-treatment, for enabling com parison with the treatment progress parameter requirement set in Sub-Step A7-3 (174) using object recognition technology. iv. Sub-Step A7-5 (175): Verifying whether or not the at least one treatment progress parameter value determined in Sub-Step A7-4 (174) complies with the parameter requirement set in Sub-Step A7-3 (173). v. Sub-Step A7-6 (176): If the verification in Sub-Step A7-5 is negative, ad justing a computation of a target state. vi. Sub-Step A7-7 (177): If the verification in Sub-Step A7-5 is positive, pro ceeding with the method. 5. Computer-implemented method according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that Step A further comprises: a. Sub-Step A8 (180): Verifying whether or not the at least one digital image is avail able in or convertible into a digital file format for 3D-printing, preferably an STL-file format. 6. Computer-implemented method according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that Step B comprises: a. Sub-Step B1 (210): Creating at least one 3D-printing file based on at least one 3D- image of the customerâs teeth and/or based on at least two 2D-images of the cus tomerâs teeth taken from different view angles. 7. Computer-implemented method according to one of the claims 3 to 6, characterized in that Step B comprises: a. Sub-Step B2 (220): Creating at least one 3D-printing file based on the target state computed in Sub-Step A6-1 (161) and/or the intermediate state computed in Sub- Step A6-2 (162). 8. Computer-implemented method according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the method further comprises Step C (300): 3D-printing the at least one dental aligner accord ing to the at least one 3D-printing file created in Step B. 9. Dental aligner (8), in particular 3D-printed on the basis of the 3D-printing file created in accordance with the computer-implemented method according to one of the claims 1 to 8, comprising at least one active or passive sensor (7) that provides position and/or location information of said dental aligner (8). 10. Dental aligner (8) according to claim 9, wherein said sensor (7) complies with at least one of the following requirements: a. The sensor (7) is fixed in shape-locking manner and/or force-locking manner and/or material-locking manner to a base body of the dental aligner (8). b. The sensor (7) is a bio-compatible device in which some or all of the sensor com ponents are made of and/or encapsulated by a bio-compatible material. 

 c. The sensor (7) is inserted, preferably fitted, into a cavity or recess provided in a base body of the dental aligner (11), said cavity or recess being optionally filled with a filling material, at least partially covering the sensor (7). d. The sensor (7) is configured to determine whether or not the dental aligner (8) is seated in position in the patientâs mouth. e. The sensor (7) is configured as a humidity sensor and/or as an optical sensor and/or as a temperature sensor and/or as a pH sensor and/or as an inductive and/or capacitive position and/or pressure sensor. f. The sensor (7) is a scannable code or microchip, for example an RFID tag activated by the scan of an RFID reader before the dental aligner (1) is placed in and after the dental aligner (1) is removed from the patientâs mouth. 11. Dental aligner (8) according to claim 9 or 10, wherein the position of the sensor (7) com plies with at least one of the following requirements: a. The sensor (7) is positioned on a surface of the dental aligner (8), preferably flush with the adjoining surface of the dental aligner (8). b. The sensor (7) is positioned to be embedded within a base body of the dental aligner (8). c. A position of the sensor (7) along an arcuate longitudinal extension of the dental aligner (8) is such that the sensor (7) is located in a front part of the dental aligner (8) configured to be seated on the incisor teeth. d. A position of the sensor (7) along an arcuate longitudinal extension of the dental aligner (8) is such that the sensor (7) is located in a middle part of the dental aligner (8) configured to be seated on the canine and premolar teeth. e. A position of the sensor (7) along an arcuate longitudinal extension of the dental aligner (8) is such that the sensor (7) is located in a rear part of the dental aligner (8) configured to be seated on the molar teeth. f. A position of the sensor (7) in a widthwise direction of the dental aligner (8) is such that the sensor (7) is provided on or in an inner side wall of the dental aligner (8) configured to abut an interior surface of the teeth. 

 g. A position of the sensor (7) in a widthwise direction of the dental aligner (8) is such that the sensor (7) is provided on or in an outer side wall of the dental aligner (8) configured to abut an exterior surface of the teeth. h. A position of the sensor (7) in a widthwise direction of the dental aligner (8) is such that the sensor (7) is provided on or in a top wall of the dental aligner (8) configured to abut an occlusal surface of the teeth. 12. Communication system, comprising the dental aligner (1) according to one of claims 9 to 11 and an external communication device (2), wherein the active or passive sensor (7) is configured to exchange position and/or location information of the dental aligner (8) with said external communication device. 13. Communication system according to claim 12, wherein the sensor and/or the external com munication device is provided with a time counter to count the time the dental aligner (8) occupies a specific position or location, in particular a position seated inside the patientâs mouth. 14. Communication system according to claim 13, wherein the external communication device (2) complies with at least one of the following requirements: a. The external communication device comprises a desktop PC and/or tablet PC and/or smartphone (2) having a processing unit to evaluate the position and/or lo cation information provided by the sensor (7). a. The external communication device (2) has a processing unit configured to period ically generate a status report on the usage, usage habits and/or utility of the dental aligner (8) based on the position and/or location information provided by the sensor (7), and preferably accordingly adjust a treatment plan available in the external communication device. b. The external communication device (2) has a processing unit for tracking the dental aligner (1) using the position and/or location information provided by the sensor (7), and is preferably configured to generate an alert in the case where the distance between the external communication device (2) and the dental aligner (8) exceeds a certain threshold. 15. Communication system according to one of claims 12 to 14, wherein the sensor (7) is configured as a code or microchip and the external communication device (2) comprises a scanner configured to scan this code or microchip every time the dental aligner (8) is in serted to and removed from the patientâs mouth.
